test_scenario: Agent resumes with RESUME=2026-04-27-add-rate-limit-logs from fresh session with no local artifacts

resume_mechanism.principle: Rebuild state from GitHub issue hierarchy; no local files
resume_mechanism.source: GitHub issues (parent + phase + artifact + task issues)

state_reconstruction_steps.step_1: Find parent issue: query agent:parent label, search for work_id in title/body
state_reconstruction_steps.step_2: Load all child issues via GitHub sub-issue relationship
state_reconstruction_steps.step_3: Check phase issue closed/open state for each phase
state_reconstruction_steps.step_4: Verify gate conditions (artifact closure, approval comments)
state_reconstruction_steps.step_5: Read Machine Data YAML to confirm work_id and detect status
state_reconstruction_steps.step_6: Determine last completed phase from issue states

critical_gates.gate_a: Phase 3 exploration CLOSED + files.csv CLOSED + open-questions EXISTS
critical_gates.gate_b: Phase 4 research CLOSED + sources.md CLOSED + open-questions no status:open
critical_gates.gate_e: Phase 7 task-graph CLOSED + all task issues created + HARD STOP
critical_gates.gate_f: Phase 10 verify CLOSED + all acceptance pass

artifact_subissues.linking: Sub-issue of phase issue via GitHub sub-issue relationship

task_issues.machine_data: work_id, kind: task, phase: implement, status, depends_on: [phase numbers]

common_fields.kind: parent | phase | artifact | task | approval | implementation-log

conditional_fields.phase: intake | worktree | exploration | research | clarification | plan | task-graph | implement | review | verify | pr

next_phase_algorithm.step_2: For each phase, check: phase_issue.state == CLOSED
next_phase_algorithm.step_3: Verify gate conditions from artifact closure states
next_phase_algorithm.special_case_phase_8: If last_completed_phase == 7, hard-stop and return RESUME handoff; do not proceed in same session

resume_vs_fresh_start.fresh_start_state_source: GitHub issue or user WORK_ITEM input

critical_rules: No local artifact files (.coding-workflow/work/<slug>/) are accessed or assumed to exist
All state reconstruction happens from GitHub issues alone
Gate E (Phase 7 complete) triggers hard-stop; Phase 8 resumes in fresh session
Do not restart earlier phases unless GitHub artifact state indicates incompleteness
Machine Data YAML blocks are machine-readable phase/artifact/task tracking
skill_specification_references: SKILL.md:68 (Routing precedence: RESUME rebuilds state from GitHub issue hierarchy)
README.md:263 (Example 5: agent finds issues by work_id and determines last completed phase)
artifact-schema.md:144-151 (Resume expectations: reconstruct from GitHub alone, no local files)
workflow.md:236 (Phase 8 Step 1: resolve RESUME by loading parent and phase/task issues)
workflow.md:14 (Priority rule #4: hard-stop after Gate E, resume in fresh session)
result_summary: The skill clearly specifies that RESUME state is reconstructed entirely from GitHub issue hierarchy by looking up the parent issue via work_id, reading all child/artifact/task issues, checking their closed/open state, and reading Machine Data YAML blocks to verify phases. No local files are assumed or required. The next phase is determined by finding the last completed phase from issue states and gate conditions, then resuming from the next incomplete phase. Special handling exists for Phase 8: after Gate E passes, hard-stop and hand off RESUME command; do not proceed in same session.
key_insights: RESUME is stateless from agent perspective: all state in GitHub
Work_id encoded in parent issue Machine Data section
Phase completion detected from issue closed/open state + gate verification
Machine Data YAML in every issue enables resumable, machine-parseable workflow
No local files = same agent or different agent can resume seamlessly
Hard-stop after Phase 7 ensures implementation work (Phase 8) starts fresh
